slides.title: Happy April Fools' Day!
slides.body_text: Sniffing the glue that holds the Internet together

slides.title: Happy April Fools' Day!
slides.body_text: Sniffing the glue that holds your system together

slides.title: Happy Birthday, Wireshark!
slides.body_text: On this day in 1998, the first version of Ethereal (later renamed Wireshark) was released. Thank you to the community that has kept the project thriving ever since!

slides.title: SharkFest'26 US
slides.body_text: Join the annual Wireshark developer and user conference for talks, training, and community.

slides.title: SharkFest'26 EU
slides.body_text: Join the annual Wireshark developer and user conference for talks, training, and community.

slides.title: Endace
slides.body_text: Endace's purpose-built appliances let you capture, store, and investigate every packet at scale

slides.title: Google
slides.description_sub: Thank you for supporting the Wireshark foundation!
slides.body_text: Thank you, Google, for supporting the Wireshark foundation and the open source community.

slides.title: Profitap
slides.description_sub: Take a look at their IOTA and ProfiSharks
slides.body_text: Helping you trust every packet with purpose-built packet capture and analysis solutions

slides.title: Recall AI
slides.description_sub: The API to get transcripts, recordings, and metadata from meetings.
slides.body_text: The infrastructure behind all your favorite meeting recording products, Recall AI provides a powerful API to get transcripts, recordings, and metadata from meetings.

slides.title: Quick Filter Shortcut
slides.description: Right-click any field to apply as filter
slides.body_text: You can also middle-click to append to the current filter, or Shift+click to create a negated filter.

slides.title: Follow the Stream
slides.description: Right-click a packet and select Follow > TCP Stream
slides.description_sub: Works with UDP, TLS, and HTTP too
slides.body_text: Following a stream reassembles the full conversation between two endpoints, making it easy to read application-layer data.

slides.title: Coloring Rules
slides.description: Customize packet colors by protocol or filter
slides.body_text: Coloring rules highlight packets that match a display filter, making patterns and anomalies easy to spot at a glance.

slides.title: Export Specific Packets
slides.description: File > Export Specified Packets to save a subset
slides.description_sub: Filter first, then export only what you need
slides.body_text: Combine display filters with packet export to create small, focused capture files — perfect for sharing in bug reports or with colleagues.

slides.title: Protocol Statistics
slides.description: Statistics > Protocol Hierarchy for traffic overview
slides.description_sub: See which protocols dominate your capture
slides.body_text: The Protocol Hierarchy window breaks down your capture by protocol, showing packet counts and byte percentages at each layer.
